message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The detection of SARS-CoV-2 RNA by real-time polymerase chain reaction (PCR) in respiratory samples from COVID-19 patients is not a direct indication of the presence of viable viruses. The isolation of SARS-CoV-2 in cell culture system however, can acts as surrogate marker of infectiousness. Cell culture based studies performed mostly with hospitalized and moderate/severe COVID-19 claims that no replication competent virus is found after 9 days of the symptoms onset in respiratory samples. Therefore, it is now recommended 10 days isolation before patient discharge.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    We cell-cultured 29 SARS-COV-2 RT-PCR positive respiratory samples at the 10
                    <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                    day after the illness in Vero E6 cells. After two passages, cytopathic effect and cycle threshold (CT) lower than the obtained in the original sample were used to determine positivity.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Findings</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We found viable particles in (7/29) 24% of samples tested. The positivity in cell culture was strongly associated (p<0.0001) to the low cycle thresholds in clinical samples (Ct <21).</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>This data adds important knowledge to the current protocols for de-isolation of patients with non-hospitalized mild COVID-19.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Discontinuation of isolation for persons with COVID-19: Is 10 days really safe?

reference.unstructured: Center for Diseases Control and Prevention. Duration of Isolation and Precautions for Adults with COVID-19. Available at: https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/if-you-are-sick/end-home-isolation.html. Assessed 10 January, 2021.

reference.article-title: SARS-CoV-2, SARS-CoV-1 and MERS-CoV viral load dynamics, duration of viral shedding and infectiousness: a living systematic review and meta-analysis
Predicting infectious SARS-CoV-2 from diagnostic samples
Clinical and virologic characteristics of the first 12 patients with coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) in the United States
reference.author: The COVID-19 Investigation Team

reference.article-title: Associations of clinical characteristics and treatment regimens with the duration of viral RNA shedding in patients with COVID-19

reference.unstructured: Basile K , McPhie K , Carter I , et al. Cell-based culture of SARS-CoV-2 informs infectivity and safe de-isolation assessments during COVID-19. Clin Infec Dis, 2020;ciaa1579
